data.title: Bitcoin's next true expansion cycle may not arrive until 2026, as expert predicts high fear, volatility in the market with recent drop below $85,000 and Fear & Greed sentiment gauge at record-low 11.
data.description: According to Alice Liu, the Head of Research at CoinMarketCap, the cryptocurrency market is currently experiencing a period of heightened fear and volatility. Liu warns that Bitcoin's next significant growth cycle may not occur until 2026. During an interview at Blockchain week, Liu mentioned that Bitcoin's price fell below $85,000, leading to a record-low Fear & Greed sentiment gauge of 11, indicating "extreme fear" among investors. The anticipated bull run in October did not materialize as liquidity in the market remains low, resulting in exaggerated price fluctuations. For more information, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Luxury lodging in Hermann, Seven Acres Escape, invested $2 million in local builders & artisans, blending modern luxury with community partnership.
data.description: Seven Acres Escape, a luxurious A-frame vacation rental located near Hermann, is a testament to the collaboration between the community and exceptional design, harmonizing contemporary luxury with a sense of local unity. data.title: Neurosurgeon Dr. Aaron Cohen-Gadol launches ATLAS Institute of Brain & Spine in Los Angeles, known for minimally invasive techniques.
data.description: Dr. Aaron Cohen-Gadol, a highly esteemed neurosurgeon who received the top award in neurosurgery and established the widely known Neurosurgical Atlas, has revealed the launch of the ATLAS Institute of Brain & Spine in Los Angeles. This new facility will offer Dr. Cohen-Gadol's innovative minimally invasive techniques to patients seeking advanced neurological care.

data.title: Nasdaq edges up, small caps lead with Dollar General and AppLovin breaking out. Jobless claims surprise weekly. Robinhood at key level.
data.description: Today in the stock market, small-cap stocks led the upward trend while the Nasdaq saw a slight increase. This was driven by a surprise drop in weekly jobless claims. Dollar General and AppLovin both experienced significant growth. The Nasdaq in particular showed promising growth, with Robinhood also hitting a key level. This live coverage provided updates on the day's market performance, as reported by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Blue Gold Limited appoints Rob Simpson as Director of Risk & Digital Operations; Simpson known for work at Bolt.
data.description: In New York City on December 4, 2025, Blue Gold Limited (Nasdaq: BGL) revealed the selection of Rob Simpson as the Director of Risk and Digital Operations. Simpson, renowned for his expertise in financial risk and operational efficiency, notably held the position of Head of Risk and Payments at Bolt. This fintech giant is recognized for its rapid expansion and global influence. Simpson's appointment marks a significant move for Blue Gold, positioning them to capitalize on his proven track record in payments infrastructure and risk management.

data.title: DOGE & SHIB in spotlight, DOGE could reach $10 with $1.62 trillion market value, while SHIB faces supply wall.
data.description: Dogecoin (CRYPTO: DOGE) and Shiba Inu (CRYPTO: SHIB) have returned to the spotlight as new valuation calculations suggest that one meme coin could potentially reach $10, while the other is constrained by a supply barrier that makes achieving this target practically impossible. Dogecoin's circulating supply stands at around 161.6 billion tokens, meaning a $10 price would necessitate a market value of approximately $1.62 trillion. This figure puts the goal in the vicinity of Bitcoin's (CRYPTO: BTC) current valuation but well above the majority of global equity markets. Additionally, DOGE's fixed inflation rate of about 5 billion coins annually further elevates this threshold over time. Analysts highlight... (Full article accessible on Benzinga.com)

data.title: Greenbacker reports growth in revenue, delivering 693,000 MWh clean power and achieving construction milestone in NY's largest solar project.
data.description: A company reports an increase in revenue from independent power producer (IPP) projects compared to the previous year. They have successfully provided 693,000 megawatt-hours (MWh) of clean energy. The company has also reached a significant milestone in the construction of their primary solar project, which is set to be the largest in New York State.

data.title: Hong Kong banks roll out new assistance for Tai Po fire victims: expedited account handling & flexible credit support.
data.description: Following the recent fire incident in Tai Po, Hong Kong banks have implemented new initiatives to assist impacted individuals. These measures include expediting account services and providing flexible credit support. The move aims to aid those affected by the event and facilitate their financial needs during this challenging time.

data.title: Stagwell unifies communication and advocacy companies under Zac Moffatt, new Global Chair, to enhance tech strategies.
data.description: Stagwell, a transformative marketing challenger network traded on NASDAQ under the symbol STGW, disclosed on December 4, 2025, plans to establish a new vertical leadership team in New York and Washington. This move aims to capitalize on their decade-long track record in creating technology-driven communication strategies.

data.title: Snowflake Q3 bookings up 50%, but stock falls; analysts maintain ratings with increased price targets. RPO at $7.9B, bookings at nearly $1B, AI ARR $100M. Product revenue growth slightly lower than expected.
data.description: Snowflake Inc.'s (NYSE: SNOW) shares tumbled despite positive third-quarter results. Analysts' ratings and price targets for SNOW were revealed: Cantor Fitzgerald raised target to $278; Scotiabank to $290; Canaccord Genuity to $270; KeyBanc Capital Markets to $285; JPMorgan to $268; Goldman Sachs to $275; Wedbush to $270; DA Davidson to $300; BTIG to $312; Needham to $280; Guggenheim Securities maintained Neutral; BofA Securities to $310. Cantor Fitzgerald praised Snowflake's Q3 results with $7.9b in RPO, 37.5% growth rate, and $1b in net new RPO. Scotiabank noted a slight deceleration in product revenue growth and a performance lower than last quarter. Additional details on Benzinga.com.

data.title: C3.ai's Q2 revenue of $75.1 million beat analysts' expectations, impressing investors and leading to stock rise.
data.description: C3.ai Inc (NYSE: AI) saw a rise in shares during early trading following its positive fiscal second-quarter earnings report. Analyst Dan Ives from Wedbush reiterated an Outperform rating with a price target of $20. Canaccord Genuity's Kingsley Crane maintained a Hold rating with a $16 price target. DA Davidson's Lucky Schreiner reaffirmed an Underperform rating and $13 price target. Analyst Mike Cikos from Needham kept a Hold rating on the stock. C3.ai reported total revenue of $75.1 million for the quarter, which slightly missed its guidance range but exceeded the $74.9 million consensus. The company, under new CEO Stephen Ehikian, aims to recover from previous setbacks. Despite challenges like the government shutdown, C3.ai's federal business showed positive momentum in the third quarter. Full details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: YC F25 startup Multifactor raises $15M seed round led by Nexus Venture Partners, ensuring secure online account access for humans and AI agents.
data.description: In a recent funding round, Nexus Venture Partners took the lead as demand significantly increased for a secure and shareable way to access online accounts. The San Francisco-based company Multifactor, known for their post-quantum security platform, is paving the way for the safest online account access for humans and AI agents. This surge in demand is a strong indication of the growing need for provably secure online interactions. The information was disclosed on December 4, 2025.
